edge_hypothesis: ema_rsi_atr precision variant (RSI 50-65) achieved PF=1.327 with the system's best ranging PF (2.864). The 'precision' thesis proved that NARROWING the RSI band INCREASES profit factor by filtering to higher-quality entries. This spec pushes that thesis further: what is the OPTIMAL center point? RSI 48-62 targets the equilibrium zone where momentum is perfectly balanced. In a confirmed trend (EMA_9 > EMA_21) with vol expansion (ATR > ATR_MA20), balanced RSI means the trend hasn't overheated — maximum acceleration potential remains. This is the opposite of traditional oversold/overbought RSI usage. We want RSI in the MIDDLE because that's where trend acceleration begins, not where it exhausts. The narrow band (14-point width vs default 30-point) produces fewer but theoretically higher-quality entries. At 12:1 R:R, we only need ~8% win rate — and center-band entries during confirmed trends should produce disproportionately large winners because we're catching the trend at its most elastic point. The asymmetric short bands (38-52) compensate for crypto's natural long bias — shorts need RSI closer to center because the long-bias drift means RSI spends less time in the lower-center zone. Invalidation: if total trades < 25, the center band is too narrow. If win rate < 5%, we're catching indecision (range-bound RSI) not pre-acceleration.

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_center_sniper_12to1_4h
variants.description: Center-band RSI sniper on ETH/4h with 12:1 R:R. RSI 48-62 for longs (equilibrium zone in uptrend), RSI 38-52 for shorts (equilibrium in downtrend). EMA_9 > EMA_21 + ATR > ATR_MA20 provides trend + vol confirmation. 24.0x ATR TP captures the full acceleration move from equilibrium. Fewer trades than precision variant but each entry is at the theoretical maximum-potential point.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (24.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < precision_10to1 (1.327), narrower band reduces quality not noise — center-band thesis is wrong
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 24.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_9 / EMA_21 crossover — confirmed trend direction
RSI_14 center-band 48-62 longs / 38-52 shorts — equilibrium zone for maximum acceleration potential
ATR_14 vol expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 24.0x TP — 12:1 R:R

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_center_sniper_10to1_4h
variants.description: Center-band RSI sniper on ETH/4h with 10:1 R:R. Same center-band thesis at slightly conservative R:R. The precision variant was ACCEPT at 10:1 (PF=1.327), so 10:1 is a proven sweet spot for ema_rsi_atr. If center-band (48-62) outperforms precision (50-65) at the same 10:1, the equilibrium thesis is validated — the narrower symmetric band produces better entries than the wider asymmetric one.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < precision_10to1 (1.327) at same R:R, center band is not better than precision band
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 center-band 48-62 longs / 38-52 shorts — head-to-head test vs precision band (50-65)
ATR_14 vol expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 20.0x TP — 10:1 R:R (same as precision ACCEPT)

variants.name: ema_rsi_atr_center_sniper_12to1_1h
variants.description: Center-band RSI sniper on ETH/1h with 12:1 R:R. The 1h timeframe produces more EMA crossovers and more RSI readings in the center band, generating more trade candidates. Tests whether the center-band equilibrium thesis scales to faster timeframes. More data points = more statistical power to validate or reject the hypothesis.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (24.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 on 1h, center-band entries on faster timeframe catch noise not acceleration
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 24.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 center-band 48-62 longs / 38-52 shorts — equilibrium thesis on 1h for more data
ATR_14 vol expansion gate + 2.0x stop / 24.0x TP — 12:1 R:R
